title: erica glandulosa var. bondiae
intro: Erica glandulosa var. bondiae, also known as Bell Heather, is a plant species within the heather family (Ericaceae) that is native to the island of Jersey. Bell Heather has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of coughs, colds, and respiratory issues due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving congestion, and boosting the immune system. Erica glandulosa var. bondiae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal and health-promoting properties.
